name: Crab Souffle Roll
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a 15½ x 10½ x 1¾ inches pan. Line with waxed paper, grease lightly and flour.
2. Heat butter in a saucepan over low heat until melted.
3. Stir in flour and cook, stirring constantly, until smooth and bubbly.
4. Remove from heat and stir in milk. Return to heat and bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
5. Boil for 1 minute, then remove from heat and stir in egg yolks one at a time.
6. Stir in salt, red pepper, chives, and Parmesan cheese. Let cool to room temperature, cover with plastic wrap to prevent a skin forming.
7. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
8. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites with cream of tartar until stiff but not dry.
9. Gently fold in one-third of the beaten whites into the souffle batter.
10. Fold in the remaining whites and gently mix in the crab meat filling.
11. Pour the mixture into the prepared pan and bake for 35-45 minutes, or until puffed and golden brown.
12. Loosen the souffle from the edges of the pan and invert onto a cloth-covered cooling rack.
13. Spread with the crab meat filling and roll from the narrow end. Cut desired slices when cool.
14. Cook the green onions in butter until tender and stir in the remaining ingredients until hot.
